# Source Card: Guideline on haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations

## Summary
This is the primary WHO 2024 normative guideline that revises the haemoglobin cutoffs used to define anaemia in individuals and populations. For the pediatric CDS module it establishes age/population-specific haemoglobin cutoffs (four distinct child bands from 6 months to 14 years), documents that only the 6-23 month cutoff was changed in the 2024 revision (not a single universal pediatric threshold), and specifies contextual modifiers of interpretation (elevation adjustment recommended; smoking adjustment recommended; infection/inflammation NOT adjusted). It backfills anemia-KB record id WHO2024_HB.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Age/population-specific pediatric cutoffs 6-23 mo <105, 24-59 mo <110, 5-11 y <115, 12-14 y <120 g/L (5th percentile) — locator: p.xi, Table 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:54a0ea165d0ee9fc46aafd4086e2198642e702103b23d608f1c39b1d5e161570]"
- (ev_002) Revision modified the cutoff for children 6-23 months only, not all pediatric groups — locator: p.xi, Normative statement 1.a — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:29a37643b9b66e9d34f892a3abdf27d7410ec8c2b77c876c7cd9eda313852da3]"
- (ev_003) Physiological/environmental/genetic factors and lifecycle variation must be considered — locator: Section 1 definitions narrative — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f15aa08d560e2994735a1ed6b3feb84f20f46f90543c579fbc53a34ca9032047]"
- (ev_004) Elevation adjustment recommended (increment table + equation) — locator: p.xii-xiii, Normative 2.a.1 + Table 4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4340d57f167edfcdf9e1c90ac57f34105229683254a7dd5e29e0428d2efa333a]"
- (ev_005) Haemoglobin should NOT be adjusted for infection/inflammation — locator: p.xiv, Normative 2.b — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0005b4afc53451fed023f430a6f8ffd12c4ff65ab6f146ee47e87404b4628089]"
- (ev_006) 2024 cutoffs update a traceable lineage of prior WHO guidance (1968-2005) — locator: methods narrative — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0deadcfc320c8bc3407c90713c1fc234d305dde9eb222a4fb00629dcbdf48a0e]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: apparently-healthy reference basis; pediatric bands 6-23 mo, 24-59 mo, 5-11 y, 12-14 y (girls/boys), then adults 15-65 y and pregnancy trimesters (out of pediatric scope).
- Assay/method: cutoffs are 5th-percentile statistical values on haemoglobin concentration; stated as not analyzer/method-dependent. Values are expressed in g/L (WHO), NOT g/dL; any conversion to the CDS module's g/dL units is an implementation step, not asserted here.
- Lifecycle: guideline version WHO 2024 (ISBN 978-92-4-008854-2), effective 2024; supersedes WHO 2011 and prior 1968-2005 WHO documents.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The built-in pediatric cutoff table begins at 6 months; WHO 2024 provides NO cutoff for infants <6 months or neonates, supporting the KB's SCOPE-001 neonatal/young-infant pathway boundary. It likewise does not define a within-source referral/red-flag threshold beyond the anaemia cutoff and severity bands (Table 3). High-altitude interpretation requires the elevation adjustment (ev_004), backing the KB's no-altitude-adjustment safety note.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: The explicit prior (pre-2024) numeric cutoff for children 6-23 months (commonly cited as 110 g/L) is NOT stated verbatim in the executive summary passage quoted; Normative 1.a says only that the cutoff was "modified." The revised value <105 g/L IS verbatim. The prior numeric value is therefore recorded as a gap, not asserted.
- Units: all WHO cutoffs are in g/L. The pediatric-CDS module operates in g/dL; unit conversion is deliberately left to the CDS converter and is not performed in this card.
- Locator precision: page anchors reflect the WHO IRIS PDF Executive-summary pagination (roman-numeral pages x-xviii) captured via text extraction; Table 4/5 exact page may differ by +/-1 page in some renderings.
- No conflict with the co-cited pediatric sources (AAP2026_IDA, BLOOD2022_PED_ANEMIA) was found for these passages; WHO defines population cutoffs while those sources address clinical work-up. Severity bands (Table 3) and adult/pregnancy cutoffs are present in the source but out of pediatric-CDS scope and not extracted here.
